architecture.summary: HyperGuide injects a hyperbolic geometric signal into LLM decoding to guide multi-step reasoning. A lightweight head projects the model's hidden states into hyperbolic space, where distance-to-origin encodes solution proximity and angular separation distinguishes reasoning branches. A low-rank adapter is fine-tuned via interactive learning on the model's own reasoning attempts to act on this injected geometric signal during step-by-step generation.
trained_components: lightweight projection head (hidden states → hyperbolic space)
low-rank adapter (interprets hyperbolic signal for decoding)

biblio.title: HyperGuide: Hyperbolic Guidance for Efficient Multi-Step Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: The hyperbolic space matches the asymmetry of combinatorial reasoning trees: distance-to-origin naturally encodes solution proximity while angular separation distinguishes branches requiring different next operations
The geometric signal yields consistent gains across multiple benchmarks, with larger improvements on deeper reasoning chains

In combinatorial reasoning trees, solution-bearing states are few while dead ends are exponentially numerous; hyperbolic space has compact volume near the origin and exponentially expanding capacity toward the boundary
